title: Multi-Currency Schema — currency_code on Accounts and Transactions
context: ADR-004 deferred multi-currency support for the MVP. BR-005 has now been superseded by BR-006 as the user has confirmed multi-currency accounts are a core requirement. ADR-004 anticipated this migration and noted that adding currency_code columns to accounts and transactions would be the correct path. The user has confirmed that: (1) each account has one fixed currency, (2) transactions inherit the account's currency, (3) FX transfers store both legs with their respective currencies, (4) no exchange rate is stored or calculated, (5) the existing DB can be cleared (no live user data to migrate).
decision: Add a mandatory non-null currency_code column (ISO 4217, e.g. 'EUR', 'USD') to both the accounts table and the transactions table. No separate currencies look-up table is required in the database — the valid currency list is hardcoded in the application layer as a curated set of common currencies. FX transfers are handled by the existing two-leg (debit + credit) transfer model: each leg stores its own currency_code and amount. No exchange_rate column is added.

alternatives.name: Dedicated currencies table
alternatives.description: Store currencies as DB rows with code, symbol, name, and decimal_places.
pros: Supports dynamic addition of custom currencies
Normalised — single source of truth for symbol/name
cons: Over-engineered for current scope (no custom currencies needed)
Adds a FK join to every balance query

alternatives.name: Store exchange rate on FX transfer
alternatives.description: Add an exchange_rate column to the transfer record.
cons: User confirmed no cross-currency totals are needed now
Effective rate is implied by (source_amount, target_amount) — storing it separately is redundant and potentially inconsistent

positive: Clean separation between account currency and transaction currency allows independent evolution
FX transfers work naturally with the existing two-leg model
No exchange rate complexity — the system never needs to fetch or validate rates
Per-currency balance queries are trivial (filter by account, sum by that account's currency)
negative: All existing TransactionEntity and AccountEntity Room entity classes must be updated
Room schema migration required (version bump); since no live users, a destructive migration (drop & recreate) is acceptable per user decision
All balance queries, dashboard queries, and timeline queries must be reviewed to ensure they do not aggregate across currencies
neutral: Currency symbol display logic moves from a global app constant to a per-account lookup
